_comment: Committed, hand-authored NEGATIVE and BOUNDARY candidates for the two binary
labelling tasks. Authored BEFORE the second labeller sees anything, so the
item set cannot be tuned to the answers we get back.
These are not padding. A binary agreement task needs negatives, and the
negatives have to be plausible or kappa is inflated by easy rejections. Each
• clear negatives  — clinical content nobody should redact / non-essential detail
• BOUNDARY cases   — genuinely contestable, flagged below. These are where the
rubric earns its keep, and where a disagreement is a
finding about the GOLD SET rather than about the labeller.
Two boundary cases are deliberately aimed at our own gold set: dose frequency
('three times daily', 'twice daily') is safety-critical to a patient and is NOT
currently a gold anchor in the simplification cases. If the second labeller
calls those essential, our anchors are incomplete and the paper should say so.

redaction._task: Which spans MUST be removed before this text could be shared outside the care team?

redact-cardio-clinic.note: boundary — treating clinician, not the patient

simplification._task: Which facts MUST survive into the patient-facing simplification? (Losing one would be a safety problem.)

simplify-discharge-brief.note: BOUNDARY AGAINST OUR OWN GOLD — dose frequency, not currently an anchor

simplify-lab-results.note: BOUNDARY AGAINST OUR OWN GOLD — dose frequency, not currently an anchor

simplify-medication-change.note: BOUNDARY — the OLD dose; arguably must be dropped, not kept
boundary — the reason for the change
